sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 2-year history of chronic low back pain, which started after a fall while playing basketball.
The pain is sharp and throbbing, radiating down the left leg, and is exacerbated by standing and relieved by lying on the side.
The patient has experienced numbness in the left leg and has had one episode of incontinence, which was not a consistent issue.
The patient has tried injections and physical therapy, but with limited success.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of smoking, but is currently not smoking.
The patient's father's fourth cousin has scoliosis.
The patient has gained 80 pounds in the past year.
Review of systems: The patient denies fever, chills, or other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature were not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient had midline tenderness on the L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experienced pain with lumbar flexion and extension.
The straight leg test was positive.
The patient had decreased sensation to light touch at the L4 dermatome, patella, and Achilles reflexes were symmetrical.
Test Results: MRI results showed a disk desiccation, disk bulge with paracentral disk herniation resulting in moderate right foraminal stenosis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a herniated disk at L4-L5 with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Lumbar back pain; Herniated disk at L4-L5 with radiculopathy; Moderate right foraminal stenosis.
Plan:
I recommended a spinal injection with a corticosteroid to help reduce inflammation and target the bulge.
The patient will take a prescription for a medication to help with anxiety before the procedure.
I will write a prescription for pain medication, but only if the patient still experiences pain after the injection.
Follow-up: The patient will schedule the injection procedure at the front desk, which should take about an hour.
The patient may need to repeat the procedure up to 4 times a year if the first one is successful.
